   <item>
      <title>Warriors Adding LeBron James 'Has Some Legs'</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284756/Warriors-Adding-LeBron-James-Has-Some-Legs</link>
      <description><p>The Golden State Warriors know they don't have a long time left to build a winner around Stephen Curry. That has the Warriors constantly looking for upgrades. Two seasons ago, the team added Jimmy Butler. This past trade deadline, Golden State made a run at Kawhi Leonard.</p>
<p>Now, this offseason, the Warriors may make another run at adding LeBron James. The idea of James joining Golden State reportedly "has some legs" per an NBA source.</p>
<p>The Warriors previously discussed adding James before they ultimately turned to trading for Butler. James has previously said that he would love to play with Stephen Curry, but said he might have to settle for playing on Team USA at the Paris Olympics in 2024.</p>
<p>James will be an unrestricted free agent this summer. It's unknown what the NBA's all-time leading scorer's future holds. James has acknowledged that he's closing in on the end of his career but hasn't committed to this being his last season. There remains the possibility that James re-signs with the Los Angeles Lakers, but the team could choose to open up cap space to retool the roster around Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 29 Mar 2026 16:26:00 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284756/Warriors-Adding-LeBron-James-Has-Some-Legs</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/James_LeBron_lal_260212.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>UConn's Braylon Mullins Hits 35-Foot Buzzer-Beater To Complete 19-Point Comeback, Stun Duke 73-72</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284758/UConns-Braylon-Mullins-Hits-35-Foot-Buzzer-Beater-To-Complete-19-Point-Comeback-Stun-Duke-73-72</link>
      <description><p>Braylon Mullins stole a pass near midcourt and buried a 35-foot three-pointer at the buzzer Sunday, capping a stunning 19-point comeback as UConn defeated top-overall seed Duke 73-72 in the Elite Eight. The Huskies advance to the Final Four for the third time in four years.</p>
<p>The sequence unfolded in the final seconds after UConn's Silas Demary Jr. made one of two free throws with 10 seconds remaining, cutting Duke's lead to two. The Blue Devils attempted to run out the clock, but Cayden Boozer's pass near midcourt was deflected. Mullins collected the loose ball and launched from 35 feet.</p>
<p>"It's a UConn culture, a UConn heart," coach Dan Hurley said. "We believe we're supposed to win this time of year."</p>
<p>Duke led by as many as 19 points in the first half and still held a double-digit advantage with just over six minutes remaining. Tarris Reed Jr.'s 26 points kept UConn within reach before a burst of three-pointers in the final four minutes put the Huskies ahead.</p>
<p>Cameron Boozer, the front-runner for the <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/ncaa/awards/12/Naismith-College-Player-of-the-Year">Naismith Player of the Year award</a>, finished with 27 points, eight rebounds and four assists in what was likely his final college game.</p>
<p>The collapse marks the second consecutive season Duke has surrendered a late lead in the NCAA tournament. Last year, the Blue Devils led by six with 1:14 remaining before falling to Houston in the national semifinals.</p>
<p>Mullins, a freshman who grew up just outside Indianapolis, will return home for the Final Four. The victory also carries historical resonance as it came 36 years after Christian Laettner's buzzer-beater eliminated UConn in the Elite Eight and sent Duke to the Final Four.</p>
<p>The Final Four field is now complete. UConn faces Illinois while Michigan takes on Arizona next Saturday in Indianapolis. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball/ncaab/march-madness/odds-lines/">The Huskies are 2.5 point betting underdogs against Illinois</a>. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 29 Mar 2026 21:45:18 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284758/UConns-Braylon-Mullins-Hits-35-Foot-Buzzer-Beater-To-Complete-19-Point-Comeback-Stun-Duke-73-72</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Mullins_Braylon_uconn_251212.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Anthony Edwards Expected To Return Monday After Missing Six Games With Knee Injury</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284745/Anthony-Edwards-Expected-To-Return-Monday-After-Missing-Six-Games-With-Knee-Injury</link>
      <description><p>Anthony Edwards is on track to return to action Monday at the Dallas Mavericks, giving the Minnesota Timberwolves a needed boost as the playoff race tightens in the Western Conference. Edwards was cleared Friday for all on-court basketball activities and must complete a workout Sunday and shootaround Monday before receiving final clearance for game action.</p>
<p>Minnesota fell 109-87 to the Detroit Pistons on Saturday, posting season lows in points, field goal percentage and three-point percentage while also missing Jaden McDaniels and Ayo Dosunmu to injury.</p>
<p>The fifth-seeded Wolves (45-29) sit 1.5 games behind the fourth-seeded Denver Nuggets and just a half-game ahead of the sixth-seeded Houston Rockets. </p>
<p>Edwards has appeared in 58 games but only 57 count toward awards eligibility. He must play all eight remaining regular-season games to reach the 65-game threshold required for All-NBA consideration. He has earned All-NBA Second Team honors in each of the past two seasons.</p>
<p>Without Edwards, McDaniels and Dosunmu, Minnesota shot just 32% from the field and 21% from three-point range against Detroit. The Wolves forced 20 turnovers but converted them into just seven points.</p>
<p>"We can't relax now that Ant's coming back and say, 'Hey, give Ant the keys and just go,'" point guard Mike Conley said. "I think we've got to let Ant do his thing. But at the same time, continue to push the ball, continue to involve everybody, and I think that's when we're our best self."</p>
<p>Minnesota's remaining schedule ranks among the most demanding of any team in the playoff picture, with road games at Detroit, Philadelphia, Orlando and Houston still ahead. Chris Finch described both McDaniels and Dosunmu as day to day.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 29 Mar 2026 08:10:24 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284745/Anthony-Edwards-Expected-To-Return-Monday-After-Missing-Six-Games-With-Knee-Injury</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Edwards_Anthony_min_251104.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Rick Pitino Signs Extension With St. John's, Becomes Big East's Second-Highest-Paid Coach</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284759/Rick-Pitino-Signs-Extension-With-St-Johns-Becomes-Big-Easts-Second-Highest-Paid-Coach</link>
      <description><p>Rick Pitino has signed a contract extension with St. John's that adds one year through the 2029-30 season and includes a substantial pay increase, the school confirmed Sunday. The deal makes Pitino, 73, the Big East's second-highest-paid coach behind UConn's Dan Hurley, according to ESPN sources.</p>
<p>"We're thrilled that Coach Pitino has signed a new agreement to remain at St. John's, a deal that will keep him in Queens through the end of the decade," athletic director Ed Kull said. "Coach Pitino has changed the culture of our community and we want his presence to be felt on this campus for years to come."</p>
<p>The extension follows back-to-back historic seasons for the Red Storm, who became the first program in Big East history to capture both the regular season and conference tournament titles in consecutive years. St. John's finished 30-7 this season, ranked as high as fifth nationally, before falling to Duke in the Sweet 16 on Friday.</p>
<p>Pitino holds an 81-25 record in three seasons at St. John's, with two NCAA tournament appearances and 30 or more wins in each of the past two campaigns. The Sweet 16 run was the program's first since 1999 and made St. John's the fourth program Pitino has guided to the second weekend of the tournament.</p>
<p>The Red Storm's resurgence has restored the program's standing in New York, with sold-out crowds at Madison Square Garden becoming routine and an energy not seen at the school in nearly a generation.</p>
<p>The turnaround is equally a story of Pitino's own revival. He was dismissed from Louisville in October 2017 following a federal investigation into college basketball, then rebuilt his career coaching professionally in Greece before returning stateside at Iona in 2020, where he guided the Gaels to two NCAA appearances in three seasons.</p>
<p>St. John's commitment signals continued investment in revenue sharing and NIL resources that have helped Pitino assemble competitive rosters each season.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 29 Mar 2026 21:49:08 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284759/Rick-Pitino-Signs-Extension-With-St-Johns-Becomes-Big-Easts-Second-Highest-Paid-Coach</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Pitino_Rick_ncaa_090329.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Keaton Wagler Scores 25 As Illinois Beats Iowa To Reach First Final Four Since 2005</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284742/Keaton-Wagler-Scores-25-As-Illinois-Beats-Iowa-To-Reach-First-Final-Four-Since-2005</link>
      <description><p>Illinois erased a 12-2 early deficit Saturday to defeat Iowa 71-59 in the Elite Eight at Toyota Center in Houston, advancing to the program's first Final Four since 2005 when they eventually lost to North Carolina in the national championship. Freshman Keaton Wagler led the Illini with 25 points, and a suffocating second-half defense held the Hawkeyes to 23.1% shooting after halftime.</p>
<p>Illinois shot just 3-of-17 from three-point range but dominated inside, outscoring Iowa 40-12 in the paint and finishing with a 57% offensive rebounding rate. The Illini converted 58% of their field goal attempts in the second half. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/ncaa/boxscore/2026-03-28/Iowa-at-Illinois/521857">Illinois scored 1.239 points per possession in the game compared to 102.9 for Iowa</a>.</p>
<p>Wagler's spin move and subsequent step-back three-pointer with 12:13 remaining gave Illinois a 46-44 lead it would not relinquish. His floater with 4:51 left sparked a decisive 15-8 closing run.</p>
<p>Iowa's Bennett Stirtz, the driving force behind the Hawkeyes' first Elite Eight appearance in 39 years, scored 15 first-half points but managed just two field goals after intermission as Illinois applied greater defensive pressure.</p>
<p>David Mirkovic contributed nine points and 12 rebounds. Guard Kylan Boswell, a Champaign native who grew up near the Illinois campus, described the moment in terms of childhood aspirations.</p>
<p>"That's always a little kid's dream to win a national championship or hit the game-winner or something like that," Boswell said. "So now, being in these types of moments where all these things can come to fruition and be a part of your reality, now I get a chance to go play in the Final Four in Indianapolis for my hometown team for a national championship. Yeah, that's crazy."</p>
<p>Coach Brad Underwood, who spent 26 years working toward his first head coaching position, reflected on what the program has built after cutting down the net.</p>
<p>"When you're a kid, you dream about this," Underwood said. "You start your journey and you're a junior college coach, and it took me 26 years to become a head coach. You watch everybody do it, and you wonder if your opportunity's going to come."</p>
<p>Illinois will play in Indianapolis, the site of this year's Final Four, with a national championship berth on the line.</p>
<p>"You know," Underwood said, "it's even better than I imagined it would be."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 29 Mar 2026 06:51:12 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284742/Keaton-Wagler-Scores-25-As-Illinois-Beats-Iowa-To-Reach-First-Final-Four-Since-2005</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Wagler_Keaton_il_260316.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Duke's Cayden Boozer Takes Full Blame For Turnover: I Ruined Our Team's Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284761/Dukes-Cayden-Boozer-Takes-Full-Blame-For-Turnover-I-Ruined-Our-Teams-Season</link>
      <description><p>Duke freshman point guard Cayden Boozer shouldered full responsibility Sunday for the turnover that directly led to <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284758/UConns-Braylon-Mullins-Hits-35-Foot-Buzzer-Beater-To-Complete-19-Point-Comeback-Stun-Duke-73-72">UConn's game-winning buzzer-beater in the Huskies' 73-72 Elite Eight victory</a>.</p>
<p>With 7.5 seconds remaining and Duke protecting a two-point lead, Boozer caught an inbound pass near midcourt and attempted to deliver the ball to a wide-open Pat Ngongba. UConn's Silas Demary tipped the pass with 6.4 seconds left, and Braylon Mullins retrieved the loose ball before burying a shot near the half-court logo with 1.8 seconds remaining.</p>
<p>"I could have been strong with the ball and not turned it over," Boozer said. "I take full responsibility."</p>
<p>Boozer recounted his decision-making in the moment. </p>
<p>"I saw two guys open," Boozer said. "I was just trying to get it there, but I could have taken my time. We just had a lot of time. I turned the ball over. I lost it. I ruined our team's season."</p>
<p>Teammate Dame Sarr immediately pushed back on Boozer's self-criticism, noting that Boozer had stepped into the starting lineup after Caleb Foster broke his foot March 7 and helped Duke win the ACC Tournament and its first two NCAA Tournament games.</p>
<p>"I will never let him say that again, because he didn't fail us at all," Sarr said. "He picked us up."</p>
<p>Duke coach Jon Scheyer also deflected blame from any single play or player.</p>
<p>"It's easy to look at that play - I look at every play that happened, especially in that second half," Scheyer said. "This is not about one play. It's about every play that put us in that position."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 29 Mar 2026 22:30:18 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284761/Dukes-Cayden-Boozer-Takes-Full-Blame-For-Turnover-I-Ruined-Our-Teams-Season</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Logo_Duke.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>All 20 NBA Playoff, Play-In Teams Determined After Bucks Eliminated In Loss to Spurs</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284744/All-20-NBA-Playoff-Play-In-Teams-Determined-After-Bucks-Eliminated-In-Loss-to-Spurs</link>
      <description><p><a href="https://x.com/KeithSmithNBA/status/2038004483439972823">All 20 teams competing in the NBA playoffs and play-in tournament have been determined</a> after the Milwaukee Bucks were officially eliminated from contention Saturday, though seeding remains unsettled across both conferences. The NBA regular season ends in two weeks on April 12th.</p>
<p>Milwaukee's exit came via a 127-95 loss to the San Antonio Spurs, <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/teams/Milwaukee-Bucks/16/playoff-history">ending a nine-season postseason streak</a>, which was the second-longest active run in the league behind <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/teams/Boston-Celtics/2/Playoff-History">the Boston Celtics' 11 consecutive appearances</a>.</p>
<p>The Bucks have lost nine of their past 11 games. Giannis Antetokounmpo missed his sixth straight game with a left knee hyperextension and bone bruise, having appeared in just 36 games this season. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/player/Giannis-Antetokounmpo/News/49629">The two-time MVP's availability has become entangled in broader debates</a> over tanking and the league's player participation policy. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284657/NBPA-Critical-Of-Bucks-Injury-Management-Of-Giannis-Antetokounmpo">The National Basketball Players Association issued a statement</a> earlier this week that appeared to rebuke the Bucks over their handling of Antetokounmpo's status.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284567/Bucks-Ownership-On-Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Hell-Be-Extended-Or-Hell-Be-Traded">Ownership of the Bucks have gone on the record stating that Antetokounmpo will either sign an extension with the franchise this offseason of be traded</a>.</p>
<p>Milwaukee had made the playoffs in each season since drafting Antetokounmpo in 2013, establishing the franchise as a perennial Eastern Conference contender throughout that run.</p>
<p>San Antonio, meanwhile, continues its late-season surge. The Spurs won their eighth straight game and their 13th in the past 14 to move within two games of the first-place Oklahoma City Thunder in the Western Conference.</p>
<p>Stephon Castle recorded a triple-double with 22 points, 10 assists and 10 rebounds. Victor Wembanyama, drawing increasing MVP consideration, contributed 23 points, 15 rebounds and six assists.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 29 Mar 2026 07:09:20 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284744/All-20-NBA-Playoff-Play-In-Teams-Determined-After-Bucks-Eliminated-In-Loss-to-Spurs</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Antetokounmpo_Giannis_mil_250502.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Milan Momcilovic Intends To Declare For NBA Draft</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284749/Milan-Momcilovic-Intends-To-Declare-For-NBA-Draft</link>
      <description><p>Iowa State junior forward Milan Momcilovic intends to declare as an Early Entry Candidate for the 2026 NBA Draft. Momcilovic's season ended in the Elite Eight of the NCAA Tournament when Iowa State lost to Tennessee.</p>
<p>"I probably, definitely, (will) test the draft," Momcilovic told Cyclone Alert in a conversation at the Big 12 Tournament in Kansas City. "Just because I think I've had a good year and shot the ball very well that I can."</p>
<p>In 37 games during his junior year with the Cyclones, Momcilovic averaged 16.9 point per game. The 6-foot-8 forward shot 50.6% from the field, including 48.6% on 7.6 three-point attempts per game.</p>
<p>Momcilovic made it clear that entering the Draft is not a guarantee that he'll stay in the draft. He's leaving the door open to return to school for his senior year.</p>
<p>"I don't know what feedback we'll get," Momcilovic said. "We'll see if I stay in or come back. That's still to be determined."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 29 Mar 2026 11:30:46 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284749/Milan-Momcilovic-Intends-To-Declare-For-NBA-Draft</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Logo_IowaState.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Yaxel Lendeborg Dominates As Michigan Blows Out Tennessee, Advances To First Final Four Since 2018</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284757/Yaxel-Lendeborg-Dominates-As-Michigan-Blows-Out-Tennessee-Advances-To-First-Final-Four-Since-2018</link>
      <description><p>Michigan dismantled Tennessee 95-62 Sunday at the United Center in Chicago, advancing to the program's first Final Four since 2018. The Wolverines, who set a single-season program wins record at 35, will face top-seeded Arizona on April 4 in Indianapolis and are <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball/ncaab/march-madness/odds-lines/">1.5 point betting favorites</a>.</p>
<p>Yaxel Lendeborg, named the Midwest Region's most outstanding player, led Michigan with 27 points, seven rebounds, four assists and no turnovers. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/ncaa/conferences/Big-Ten-Conference/2/awards/Historical/Conference">The Big Ten Player of the Year</a> became the first Wolverine to score 20 or more points in three straight NCAA tournament games since Juwan Howard in 1994.</p>
<p>Michigan was uneven through the opening nine minutes before erupting with a 21-0 run that effectively ended the contest. The Wolverines shot the ball with precision throughout, scoring 90 or more points for the fourth time in as many tournament games.</p>
<p>Elliot Cadeau recorded a game-high 10 assists, becoming the first Big Ten player to post seven or more assists in four consecutive NCAA tournament games since Trey Burke in 2013. Aday Mara contributed 11 points, four rebounds and two blocks despite playing much of the game with two fouls.</p>
<p>"We start off every practice pretty much with hibachi passing," Lendeborg said. "Nobody cares about their stats on this team."</p>
<p>Tennessee managed just 15 first-half points and shot 5-of-26 from three-point range. Senior guard Ja'Kobi Gillespie led the Vols with 21 points, but foul trouble hampered their frontcourt throughout.</p>
<p>The defeat dropped Tennessee to 0-3 in Elite Eight appearances under coach Rick Barnes, who last reached the Final Four with Texas in 2003.</p>
<p>"Simply, we all have to get better, we all have to keep working at it," Barnes said. "We certainly know what it takes to get here, and now we've got to figure out getting through to the next level."</p>
<p>Coach Dusty May, in just his second season after inheriting a Michigan program that won only eight games the prior year, reached his second Final Four in four seasons after previously guiding Florida Atlantic in 2023.</p>
<p>"We have a sign in our locker that [reads] 'April Habits,'" May said. "From day one, we challenged these guys to develop championship-level habits that would allow us to win a Big Ten championship, and would also allow us to turn the calendar from March until April. Now we've put ourselves in position to do that."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 29 Mar 2026 21:40:48 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284757/Yaxel-Lendeborg-Dominates-As-Michigan-Blows-Out-Tennessee-Advances-To-First-Final-Four-Since-2018</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Lendeborg_Yaxel_mich_260321.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Boston College Hires UConn Assistant Luke Murray As Head Coach</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284760/Boston-College-Hires-UConn-Assistant-Luke-Murray-As-Head-Coach</link>
      <description><p>Boston College has named UConn assistant Luke Murray its next men's basketball head coach, the school announced Thursday. Murray, widely regarded as a key architect of the Huskies' offensive system, played a central role in building back-to-back national championship teams in 2023 and 2024.</p>
<p>"I am deeply honored and incredibly grateful to lead the Boston College men's basketball program," Murray said. "Boston College alumni and fans will find our program defined by a standard of excellence, and our team will play an unselfish, tough and highly competitive brand of basketball."</p>
<p>Murray has worked under Dan Hurley at Wagner, Rhode Island, and UConn since 2021, establishing himself as a top-level recruiter and offensive architect. Before his arrival in Storrs, UConn ranked 64th and 85th nationally in adjusted offensive efficiency. Since joining Hurley's staff, the Huskies have never finished below 30th in that category, ranking first and third in their two championship seasons.</p>
<p>Hurley offered an emphatic endorsement on social media.</p>
<p>"BC is Back! This guy is the best," Hurley wrote. "Will miss him greatly but so proud and happy for him. Long overdue opportunity. Good Bye Champ!!"</p>
<p>Prior to UConn, Murray served as an assistant under Chris Mack at Xavier and Louisville, contributing to five NCAA tournament appearances, including an Elite Eight run in 2017 and a top overall seed in 2018. He began his college coaching career at Quinnipiac in 2007 and spent two seasons at Towson from 2011 to 2013.</p>
<p>Murray is the son of actor and comedian Bill Murray.</p>
<p>"In Luke Murray, we have found a leader who does not just understand the modern landscape of college basketball - he has helped define it," Boston College athletic director Blake James said. "His role in building a national championship-caliber program, his sophisticated offensive vision and his relentless pursuit of excellence make him the perfect fit."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 29 Mar 2026 22:20:47 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284760/Boston-College-Hires-UConn-Assistant-Luke-Murray-As-Head-Coach</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Logo_BostonCollege.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Arizona Freshman Trio Combines For 52 Points, Secure First Final Four Since 2001</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284743/Arizona-Freshman-Trio-Combines-For-52-Points-Secure-First-Final-Four-Since-2001</link>
      <description><p>Top-seeded Arizona ended a 24-year Final Four drought Saturday, defeating second-seeded Purdue 79-64 at SAP Center in San Jose to advance to Indianapolis. The victory gave the Wildcats their 36th win of the season, a program record surpassing the mark set by the 1988 squad under legendary coach Lute Olson.</p>
<p>Arizona's three freshman starters carried the offensive load. Koa Peat scored 20, Ivan Kharchenkov added 18 and Brayden Burries contributed 14, combining for 52 points. Senior guard Jaden Bradley added 14 more.</p>
<p>Arizona head coach Tommy Lloyd addressed the crowd afterward and invoked Olson, who died in 2020 and guided the program to four Final Fours including the 1997 national title.</p>
<p>"I know this," Lloyd said. "There's a good-looking guy with white hair looking down on us right now that's happy.</p>
<p>"Lute's given me a great life," Lloyd added. "His legacy in Tucson is so powerful that he's the guy, there's others before him, but he's the main catalyst to make our program the center of the community."</p>
<p>Arizona led 19-12 before Purdue clawed back behind sophomore center Daniel Jacobsen, a 7-foot-4 reserve who had logged just four combined minutes in the previous two games. His size briefly disrupted Arizona's interior presence, but the advantage proved temporary.</p>
<p>At halftime, Lloyd delivered an unconventional message before clearing the locker room.</p>
<p>"I said, 'Guys, the coaching staff and I are going to leave right now. You guys got a few minutes to talk amongst yourselves and kind of figure this deal out and let's go kick their ass in the second half,'" Lloyd said.</p>
<p>Arizona reclaimed the lead within five minutes of the second half and steadily extended it.</p>
<p>The loss ends the college career of Purdue guard Braden Smith, who broke Bobby Hurley's all-time NCAA assists record earlier this season and finished with 1,103. Smith scored 13 points but shot just 4-of-15 from the field.</p>
<p>Lloyd's five-year record of 148 wins at Arizona sets an NCAA mark for victories by a coach in their first five seasons, surpassing the standard established by Brad Stevens at Butler. The Wildcats carry a 13-game winning streak into Indianapolis, where they will face the winner of Sunday's Michigan-Tennessee matchup.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Doc Rivers Said Health Issues, Lack Of Depth, Giannis Antetokounmpo Rumors Undid Bucks Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284741/Doc-Rivers-Said-Health-Issues-Lack-Of-Depth-Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Rumors-Undid-Bucks-Season</link>
      <description><p>The Milwaukee Bucks were officially eliminated from postseason contention after their home loss to the San Antonio Spurs on Saturday. Following the games, Bucks coach Doc Rivers was asked about being eliminated and the reasons for the down season for Milwaukee.</p>
<p>"It's been disappointing obviously," Rivers said. "Since I've been here, I haven't had a healthy stretch, and it's been your key guys. It's been Giannis (Antetokounmpo). It's been Dame (Lillard). And you hope you can play through that, but we just haven't had the ability. This year, having only one quote-unquote star, every other team has two and three. We needed health. We were thin. We knew that before the season started and it just didn't go our way. All the talk and all that stuff probably didn't help either."</p>
<p>The last portion of that quote is a reference to the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade rumors and reporting that he was unhappy with the Bucks.</p>
<p>Rivers did say that Milwaukee had some silver linings this season.</p>
<p>"But I always try to look at silver linings and Ryan (Rollins) is one of them. Pete Nance is another one. Ous (Dieng) is one of them. And we gotta rehabilitate or get A.J. Green going again. He's a good player. He's played too many minutes. We've had no choice and I think that's put him in a tough spot and I feel bad for him at times," Rivers said. "Bobby (Portis) is Bobby. He's been a pro throughout this year. We had a great talk today about it before the game. I'm so proud of him as a leader. He tries to do the right stuff. He tries to say the right things in the locker room."</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Stephen Curry 'Running Out Of Games' To Return This Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284730/Stephen-Curry-Running-Out-Of-Games-To-Return-This-Season</link>
      <description><p>Steve Kerr said Friday the Golden State Warriors are running out of games to facilitate Stephen Curry's return from a knee injury, marking the first time the organization has publicly acknowledged the window may be closing on the star guard's season.</p>
<p>"We're not bringing him back for the Play-In game. He needs to play some games, and we need to give him a runway if this is going to work," Kerr said. "And we are running out of games, that's fair to say."</p>
<p>The Warriors listed Curry as day-to-day with what the team has termed "runner's knee." A pregame injury update Friday expressed hope that Curry would participate in 5-on-5 scrimmages in the "coming days." That same target was set last weekend before the training staff opted to allow more recovery time.</p>
<p>Golden State entered Friday at 35-38, occupying the 10th seed in the Western Conference, two games behind the Los Angeles Clippers for eighth. Nine games remain in the regular season.</p>
<p>Curry last played Jan. 30 against the Detroit Pistons. Despite the circumstances, he has remained vocal about his desire to return.</p>
<p>"That's not who we are," Curry said March 15 after a road game against the New York Knicks. "If we have stuff to play for, we play. So I'm working to get back."</p>
<p>Kerr confirmed Curry continues to rehab with the intention of playing. If Curry cannot participate in a full scrimmage next week, however, a season-ending decision appears likely.</p>
<p>Kerr attributed Curry's determination to an innate competitive drive that casual observers often overlook.</p>
<p>"It's just in his blood," Kerr said. "He'll probably be the same way when he's 78 returning from an injury that's affected his golf game. He is an absolute fierce competitor, and you don't teach that. It's just how he's built."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 28 Mar 2026 10:31:41 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284730/Stephen-Curry-Running-Out-Of-Games-To-Return-This-Season</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Curry_Stephen_gsw_250429.jpg" />
   </item>
